LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BERT BENISCH AND MASON MCDUFFIE REAL ESTATE INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE UNJUST ENRICHMENT AND UCL (SECTION 17200) CLAIMS AND OVERRULED AS TO ALL OTHER CLAIMS. UNJUST ENRICHMENT IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO RESTITUTION UNDER THE UCL BECAUSE THERE IS NOTHING TO RESTORE TO HIM. PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO AN INJUNCTION UNDER THE UCL BECAUSE THERE IS NO THREAT OF CONTINUING MISCONDUCT. THE OTHER CLAIMS WHICH ARE THE SUBJECT OF THE DEMURRER RAISE FACTUAL ISSUES THAT ARE NOT AMENABLE TO RESOLUTION ON A DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED. =(302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BERT BENISCH AND MASON MCDUFFIE REAL ESTATE INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AS TO THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES, OFF CALENDAR AS TO THE ALLEGATION OF DISGORGEMENT, DENIED AS TO THE REMAINING ALLEGATIONS SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN. NO BASIS FOR FEES IS ALLEGED. THE DEMURRER TO THE UCL CLAIM IS SUSTAINED. THE REMAINING ALLEGATIONS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/HEK)

POS; OF SERVICE ; DEFTS AMENDED UINDISPUTED MATERIAL FACT#41 FILED BY DEFENDANT GRUBER FAMILY PARTNERS LP AS TO PLAINTIFF PAPPAS, PETER

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ISSUED ITS TENTATIVE RULING BEFORE HEARING. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENTS, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT GRUBER FAMILY PARTNERS LP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT WHETHER BENISCH'S ACTIONS ARE IMPUTED ON THE DEFENDANT, WHETHER BENISCH BREACHED HIS FIDUCIARY DUTY TO PAPPAS BY DISCLOSING CONFIDENTIAL COMMUNICATIONS TO GRUBER AND, ON THE INTENTIONAL AND NEGLIGENT INTERFERENCE WITH ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE, WHETHER PLAINTIFF PAPPAS HAD A PROBABILITY OF FUTURE ECONOMIC BENEFIT. [PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CCP 473 C(G).] JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order That Deft Mason Mcduffie And Bert Benisch, Waived Their Right To A Jury By Failing To Timely Deposit Jury Fees; Memo Of P&A
Off Calendar Feb-13-2013 Text Ruling

Defendant Gruber Family Partners Lp'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-25-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Saul M. Ferster, Esq. of the Law Offices of Saul Ferster, Tel. 415.863.2678, for defendant and moving party;
Gregory Cavallo, Esq. of Shopoff Cavallo & Kirsch LLP, Tel. 415.984.1975, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The court issued its tentative ruling before hearing. The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT GRUBER FAMILY PARTNERS LP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. There are triable issues of fact whether Benisch's actions are imputed on the defendant, whether Benisch breached his fiduciary duty to Pappas by disclosing confidential communications to Gruber and, on the intentional and negligent interference with economic advantage, whether plaintiff Pappas had a probability of future economic benefit. [Prevailing party to prepare a form of order that complies with CCP 473 c(g).] Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SEAN E. PONIST OF PETERSON, MARTIN & REYNOLDS, TEL. NO. 399-2900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS BERT BENISCH AND MASON MCDUFFIE REAL ESTATE INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained without leave to amend as to the unjust enrichment and UCL (section 17200) claims and overruled as to all other claims. Unjust enrichment is not a cause of action. Plaintiff is not entitled to restitution under the UCL because there is nothing to restore to him. Plaintiff is not entitled to an injunction under the UCL because there is no threat of continuing misconduct. The other claims which are the subject of the demurrer raise factual issues that are not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. =(302/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SEAN E. PONIST OF PETERSON, MARTIN & REYNOLDS, TEL. NO. 399-2900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS BERT BENISCH AND MASON MCDUFFIE REAL ESTATE INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Granted as to the request for attorney's fees, off calendar as to the allegation of disgorgement, denied as to the remaining allegations sought to be stricken. No basis for fees is alleged. The demurrer to the UCL claim is sustained. The remaining allegations are adequately pled. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Not reported. =(302/HEK)
